author: Melissa Lam

title: Fairytale Itinerary
post:
"Fairytale" activities have ended on July 10, 2007. The summary is as following:

1. By 10 days, the number of applying "Fairytale" reached 3,000.

2. After an eight-day screening process, 1100 people were selected, who came from Anhui, Beijing, Chongqing, Fujian, Gansu, Guangdong, Guangxi, Heilongjiang, Hainan, Henan, Hebei, Hubei, Hunan, Jiangsu, Liaoning, Inner Mongolia, Shanxi, Shaanxi, Shanghai, Sichuan , Tianjin, Jiangxi, Shandong, Xinjiang, Yunnan, Zhejiang. The oldest participant was 69 years old, the youngest was two and a half years old.

3. A total of 1004 people successfully received a German visa while 93 waived and 3 were refused.

4. Except 12 people gave up before the trip began, we eventually arrived at Kassel with 995 participant. Before the dropout of the 17 people, the first group had 184 people, the second group had 228, the third group had 192 people, the fourth group had 185 people and the fifth group had 189 people, respectively.

5. The progress of the project:

August 1, 2006: Visited exhibition venue in Kassel.

August 7, 2006: Promenaded around Swiss Rose Valley and encountered an Italian hiking group and initiated the prototype of "Fairytale".

January 18 -- January 21, 2007: Returned to Kassel, Germany and took part in curatorial party talks and selected the venue.

February 22, 2007: "Fairytale" documentary film crew began filming.

February 26, 2007 : "Fairytale" registration information published on the blog.

February 26 -- March 1, 2007: Bag, bamboo pillow, and mat factory visits to Jiangsu, Zhejiang, Shanghai.

March 2, 2007: Went to antique markets to select 1001 chairs that were made in Ming and Qing Dynasty styles.

March 7, 2007: Notified the deadline of scheduled registration was March 31, and later changed the deadline to March 8. 3,000 people signed up and 1100 people were selected.

March 16, 2007: Announced the list of the 1001 participants in the blog.

March 19 -- March 20, 2007: Notified each selected participant through emails.

March 20, 2007: "Fairytale" working group summarized answers about passports, visas and related issues.

March 20 -- April 9, 2007: Organized 1001 participants' passport information, and assisted them in completing electronic visa application forms, applying individual insurance, and booking flights.

March 30, 2007: Decided the order to produce suitcases.

April 10 -- May 10th, 2007: Fairytale participants and studio staff arrived at Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Chengdu German consulate in groups to apply for German visas. Except three mainland Chinese nationals were rejected, 1004 got their visas approved.

April 11, 2007: Decided to purchase kitchen appliances.

April 16, 2007: Decided the production of "Fairytale" clothing.

April 20, 2007: Decided the production of pillow covers and towel blankets.

April 30, 2007 : Decided the production of F1001 wrist bands and flash drives.

May 10, 2007: Decided the production of dormitory curtains for diving space.

May 21, 2007: Staff team went to Germany.

June 12 -- June 20, 2007: The first "Fairytale" group which consisted 183 people arrived in Germany.

June 19 -- June 25, 2007: The second "Fairytale" group which consisted 228 people arrived in Germany.

June 24 -- June 30, 2007: The third "Fairytale" group which consisted 192 people arrived in Germany.

June 29 -July 5, 2007: The fourth "Fairytale" group which consisted 185 people arrived in Germany.

July 4 -- July 10, 2007: The fifth "Fairytale" group which consisted 189 people arrived in Germany.

July 11 -- September 1, 2007: 995 people who went to Kassel, Germany all returned.

July 11-- September 1, 2007: Finished "Fairytale" documents organizing and editing the documentary clip.

---------

《童话》活动于2007.7.10结束，现总结如下：

1．《童话》通过10天网络报名人数达到3000多人。

2．经过8天筛选，入选1100人，他们来自 安徽、北京、重庆、福建、甘肃、广东、广西、黑龙江、海南、河南、河北、湖北、湖南、江苏、辽宁、内蒙古、山西、陕西、上海、四川、天津、江西、山东、新疆、云南、浙江。年龄最大者69岁，最小的是2岁半。

3．除93人自动放弃和3人被拒签外，共有1004人顺利拿到德国签证。

4．除12人出发前放弃外，最终抵达卡塞尔人数995人。分别是前期17人，第一组184人，第二组228人，第三组192人，第四组185人，第五组189人。


5．工作进展

2006年8月1日前往卡塞尔考察展览场地

2006年8月7日瑞士玫瑰谷徒步时偶遇意大利徒步团萌生《童话》雏形

2007年1月18日——1月21日再次前往德国卡塞尔与策展方会谈，选定驻地。

2007年2月22日摄制组开机《童话》记录片

2007年2月26日在博客上发布《童话》报名信息

2007年2月26日——3月1日前往江苏、浙江、上海考察箱包、竹枕、凉席等工厂

2007年3月2日去古玩市场挑选1001把明清椅

2007年3月7日通知原定的报名截止日期为3月31日，后改为3月8日。报名3000人，入选1100人

2007年3月16日在博客上公布1001人初选名单

2007年3月19日-20日给每位入选者以E-MAIL的形式发入选通知

2007年3月20日《童话》工作组总结回答了有关办理护照及签证的相关问题

2007年3月20日—4月9日整理1001人的护照信息，协助填写签证电子申请表，办理个人保险、预订机票

2007年3月30日确定生产行李箱

2007年4月10日——5月10日童话成员及工作人员分批在北京、上海、广州、成都办理赴德签证。除3人遭拒签外，签证共通过1004人

2007年4月11日确定购买厨房用具

2007年4月16日确定制作《童话》服装

2007年4月20日确定制作枕巾和毛巾被

2007年4月30日确定制作F1001手腕U盘

2007年5月10日确定制作宿舍隔断用帘子

2007年5月21日前期工作人员赴德

2007年6月12日—6月20日 第一批《童话》成员183人抵德

2007年 6月19日—6月25日第二批《童话》成员228人抵德

2007年6月24日—6月30日 第三批《童话》成员192人抵德

2007年6月29日—7月5日 第四批《童话》成员185人抵德

2007年7月4日—7月10日 第五批《童话》成员189人抵德

2007年7月11日前往德国卡塞尔995人全部返回。

2007年7月11日——9月1日童话后期整理及《童话》记录片剪辑

author: Mimi Cheng, Aaron Levy

title: Some details about the Fairytale Kassel Trip
post:
Applicant [Name removed to protect participant] has already passed through the primary selection process by the organizational committee for the "Fairytale" project and will be a part of the "Fairytale" piece. After the confirmation date, you can begin to apply for a personal passport at your perminant residence. Now, we will tell you some details about the Fairytale Kassel Trip:

1. Participants should thoroughly and clearly read all terms of participation in the Fairytale Kassel Trip, sign their name at the bottom and return via email by March 20 to organizers; overdue return signifies voluntary withdrawl from qualification.
2. After confirming participation, please do not send in your personal passport; visa application will be organized collectively by the project organizers who will then schedule and inform participants to go themselves to the German Embassay for their interview at the time scheduled by the organizers.
3. Participants can choose: □Beijing □Shanghai □Guangzhou □Chengdu; participant chooses □√ Beijing □Shanghai departure.
4. Visa application fees for participants are 600 RMB per person; whether the applicant is successfully granted a visa or not, this expense will be born by the organizers. Other costs to the participants, such as food and lodging during the time of the visa interview are the responsibility of the participants themselves.
4. Participant will be assigned to the fourth group, for the period between July 1 and July 8. Each group's time period is subject to change dependent upon actual visa and flight times.
5. Dependent on the group's schedule, participant will go to Beijing or Shanghai to pick up luggage and registration prepared by the organizers.
6. At the time of the visa interview, a detailed itinerary and specific information about luggage to be received and place of registration will be given in a supplemental notice.
7. Those participants coming from places outside of Beijing or Shanghai must take care of their own board and lodging while in Beijing and Shanghai.
8. Organizers will provide every participant with a portion of luggage including a trolley suitcase, a set of toiletries, and a jacket, all encoded in special numerical issue to be returned in original to the organizers after the conclusion of the trip. If it is necessary to be mailed, the cost will be borne by the organizing committee.
9. Organizers will be responsible for the fees of each participant in the activity, including: round-trip transportation between Beijing or Shanghai and Kassel, Germany; room and board fees while in Germany.
10. Participants will be responsible for costs borne to them in Chinese territory prior to boarding and after landing.
11. Organizers will then provide participants with a basic introductory guide to Kassel and Kassel's Documenta.
12. The flight from Bejing/Shanghai to Frankfurt is approximately 10 hours long; if flights depart during the day, flights will arrive on the same day. Bus or train travel between Frankfurt and Kassel is approximately 1.5 hours; participants must ensure that their health is suitable for long-distance travel.
13. In addition to the international flight requirements covered in their "Traveler Notifications," we remind travelers that they are not to carry beverages aboard.
14. Organizers will provide two-story factory-style accommodation for participants while they are in Kassel. Open space upstairs and downstairs for each participant is 60 meters long by 20 meters wide by 4 meters high. Men and women will be divided by floor. Each participant will receive a standard-size bed 2 meters long by 0.8 meters wide with a complete linen set.
15. Organizers will delegate a full-time chef and provide a collective meal package. Breakfast – Chinese style congee; lunch – sandwich-like fast food; dinner – Chinese-style set meals with assorted vegetables, fruit and dairy products.
16. While in Kassel, participants can choose walking as their mode of transportation; if participants choose an alternate means of transport, it will be at their own expense.
17. If participants are GSM mobile phone customers, they will maintain normal use of their cellphones while in Kassel. However, purchasing calling cards for local calls is cheaper and the organizing committee office will have a landline available for use at local rates; participants may use this at their own cost during specific hours.
18. Kassel Internet cafes charge between 2 and 4 Euros per hour; the organizing committee office will provide necessary network services to participants during specific hours.
19. A sleep schedule is suggested but not mandated for between 10PM and 9 AM.
20. Participants should bring their own pocket money for the period that they are abroad; Euro-to-RMB exchange rate is approximately 1: 10.
21. Participants should prepare their own everyday personal items for the trip, including dental equipment, towels, soap, etc.
22. Should participants wish to wash laundry while abroad, they can wash it themselves, or can also go to a commercial laundry service where washing and drying will cost about 5 Euros; this would be their own expense.
23. Approximate daily costs in Kassel: a bottle of water is over 2 Euros, sandwiches costs between 3-5 Euros, beer is 3 Euros/bottle. You can expect to spend roughly 10-15 Euros daily; 8 days x 10 Euros = 80 Euros. As the organizers will be providing basic necessities, this allowance is not necessary and could be saved.
24. The organization committee will provide an emergency phone for participants while in Germany along with the phone number for the Chinese consulate in Germany, the organizer office in Kassel and local emergency service numbers.
25. Participants can bring a few commonly-used pharmaceuticals.
26. Local volunteers will provide free translation services for tours of Kassel as well as tours of Documenta.
27. Average temperatures in June in Kassel are between 11℃-23℃; temperatures in July are between 13℃-24℃. Nights are cooler; participants are advised to bring a thin sweater and long-sleeved shirt.
28. Organizers hope that each participant will enjoy full freedom and comfort while on the Kassel trip, but participants' personal behavior should not affect others.
29. For the period that participants are abroad, they are not to leave the group to go to other cities or regions; they cannot overstay beyond the project and must depart and return with the group. Any party in breach of contract will bear the resulting economic losses and possible legal liabilities.
30. While in Kassel, participants' valuables can be handed over to the organizing committee for safekeeping; participants must care for any other belongings themselves, and any loss of property is at their own risk.
31. All participants must turn over their passports to the organizers for safekeeping, and will be given back after return to China.
32. Obligations participants going to Kassel must fulfill include: agreeing that organizers can use the first information page of participants' passport and can photograph participants' portrait. Organizers reserve the right for use of these for future artistic creation and publishing needs.
33. Participants will meet the organizers needs for documentary footage and interviews.
34. Organizers will need translators, tour guides, cleaners, kitchen help etc. and will interview volunteers who wish to register for participation.
35. Organizers hope to collect clothing (a set) used by participants while in Kassel; after the event, when returning luggage to organizers, participants can offer this as a free gift to organizers.
36. Organizers have put much effort into this trip to Kassel; each participant should diligently operate in accordance with the work of the organizers.
37. I have read and clearly understand these rules described; I agree to these rules, and to voluntarily be a part of the "Fairytale" project.
38. If participants must relinquish their right to participation, please attach your grounds for refusal.
39. The power of interpretation of these rules for the "Fairytale project" rest entirely with the organizing committee.
snippet:
Applicant [Name removed to protect participant] has already passed through the primary selection process by the organizational committee for the "Fairytale" project and will be a part of the "Fairytale" piece. After the confirmation date, you can begin to apply for a personal passport at your perminant residence. Now, we will tell you some details about the Fairytale Kassel Trip:

1. Participants should thoroughly and clearly read all terms of participation in the Fairytale Kassel Trip, sign their name at the bottom and return via email by March 20 to organizers; overdue return signifies voluntary withdrawl from qualification.
2. After confirming participation, please do not send in your personal passport; visa application will be organized collectively by the...
author: FAKE Design

title: Introducing the Fairytale Project research archive
post:
We are happy to present to you the Fairytale Project research archive. All documents, questionnaires and photographs currently on this web archive were created in 2007 on the occasion of Ai Weiwei's participation in Documenta 12, Kassel Germany.

To begin your experience in looking through Fairytale Project, we have included the initial translated letter sent to the Fairytale Participants by FAKE Design upon completing the first round of the selection process. After this letter, participants were responsible for applying for their own passport, visa, as well as filling out a questionnaire made up of 99 questions poised by Ai Weiwei. These 99 questions you will be able to find on each fairytale participant's page. Fairytale Project is slowly going through the process of translating these 1001 questionnaires, along with other documents, into English and German.

As the translation continues, so the fairytale unfolds for more of us.

----

Fairytale in Kassel Notice
 
XXX: Hello! 
 
You've already made it through the Fairytale in Kassel initial selection and can begin to apply for a personal passport.
 
With the notice you will be sent a visa registration form, regulations, and a questionnaire; please be sure to carefully read and truthfully fill out each as requested in the documents and return signed via email by March 20 to: aiweiwei.d12@gmail.com to confirm your participation in Fairytale in Kassel. If your materials are incomplete after the specified time (visa application, regulations, questionnaires all must be included), you will seen as having voluntarily relinquished your participation in Fairytale in Kassel. Please take this seriously and reply as soon as possible.
 
After you confirm, apply for a personal passport; we will do our best to cooperate with you and help you in applying for your visa. Once you pass your visa interview at the German Embassy you will be able to make the trip.
 
Should you have any questions, please contact the organization committee by phone: 010-84564194

author: Melissa Lam

title: Ai Weiwei - Unpublished Fairytale Interview (2007)
post:
Caption: Ai Weiwei and 1 of the 5 groups that went to Kassel

作为作品的"童话"
Regarding the work "Fairytale"

为什么是1001人？
Why 1001 people?

在瑞士爬山，看到大队的意大利游人从身旁走过，拖儿带女的，我就想到带一些牵肠挂肚的中国人来做一次旅行。这更像是一个完整蛋糕切开来的一块，在这一块中包含着蛋糕所有位置的特征元素。这也是为什么不是50个或100个人的原因，数量要多到一定程度。这么多人的身份特征、年龄特征、来自几乎全部省份地区的特征，影响了文化发展交流的特征。
When I was hiking in Switzerland, I came across a huge group of Italian tourists, with their families and children in tow. The idea came to me of bringing a few Chinese people on a trip. It would be like cutting an entire cake down into one piece, and that one piece would contain all of the characteristic elements of the cake. It's also why the number of people is not 50, or 100, the number had to be large enough. The individual identities of this many people, their ages, the characteristics of almost all of the regions and provinces [from which they came], and their impact on cultural development and exchange [on each person].

你会对其中的哪些人特别感兴趣？
Which people within this group were you especially interested in?

对所有活着的人，能睁开眼睛的人，想出去呼吸新鲜空气的人，晚上回来脚会疼的人，要加床被子要不感觉会冻死的人，都觉得非常有兴趣。参与者也会遇到如何在新的环境中迅速地找到自己的位置和方式的问题。你要了解你所处的群体，以及不同群体之间有怎样的关系。只有你看到了白人或黑人，你才会知道黄种人是什么样。
In all living people, in those whose eyes can be opened, those who want to go out and breathe fresh air, those whose feet hurt when they return home in the evening, those who add a quilt to their bed because they don't want to feel like they are freezing to death; I find all of these people very interesting. The participants would also have to be able to adjust to a new environment quickly and be able to figure out questions about their location and how they should behave on their own. If you want to understand the situation of your own group you must have this type of relationship with other groups. Only when you have seen white people or black people will you understand what "yellow race" people are like.

参与《童话》的人回答了99个问题的问卷，问题涉及宗教、信仰、东西方文化差别等，为什么设置这个问卷？
The participants of Fairytale each answered a questionnaire of 99 questions, which encompassed topics like religion, faith, cultural differences between the east and the west, etc., why did you choose to make this questionnaire?

问卷是一个阶段性的技术手段。我希望人们确认我们是认真地工作，不是玩笑。引导人们对西方的文化背景和个人的处境问题开始思考，这与整个作品的品质有关。农民就写"不知道"。只要他回答完这个问卷，后面签了名，说明他认同参加这个活动。这必须是自愿的。
The questionnaire is a technical measure taken in stages. I hope that the participants have worked on it seriously; it is not a joke. [I will use] their background in Western culture and each person's questions about the situation as a guide to begin thinking, and this will have a lot to do with the quality of the work itself. A farmer may give "I don't know" as an answer. So long as he or she completes the questionnaire and signs at the end of it, he or she has given the consent to participate in the event. This must be voluntary.

与《童话》相关的项目，都开展了哪些工作？
What sort of work did you have to do to prepare for "Fairytale"?

做了一个纪录片，国内外参与的导演近二十人，拍摄了《童话》中的比较有特征的人。记录他们在中国的工作、生活、日常经验，在参与过程中所付出的精神、物质上的种种努力和代价。这包括一些区域群体的生活状态，他们的期待和焦虑，愿望和现状，教育和家庭。素材已经超过一千多个小时。还会出书，一本是参与《童话》的人物访谈，让他们更完整地说出这个时期中国人的心情，另外一本是图片纪录。我不知道会做成什么，我基本上不会想得太远。
We have made a documentary, which involved close to 20 local and foreign directors, to capture the more unique participants of Fairytale. We recorded their work, life and daily encounters as well as the mental and material efforts expended during the course of participation. The living conditions of some regional communities have been included, their expectations and anxieties, their dreams and realities, their education and family situations. We already have more than one thousand hours of material. We will still come out with two books, one volume will include interviews and discussions with the participants of "Fairytale," which will allow them to more completely explain the frame of mind of Chinese people; the other volume will be a photographic record. I don't know what the finished product will be, I basically won't be thinking about it too much.

还有给每人发了统一的手提箱。
You also gave every person a suitcase.

对，另外做了一些产品。我不希望成为一个艺术的表现，这有悖于作品本身的含义。我想尽量不要打搅日常的状态，保持原有的习惯、节奏。同时，又应该有参与特殊活动的认同感。有些东西必须要有，比如床、单子、毯子、枕头、隔断，要使屋子成为有隐私的小空间。在这个陌生的城市里生活，一些构成历史记忆和特殊身份的器物很重要，比如1001把椅子、旅行箱、USB手环。这些是认证身份的象征，也与一定的自信和优越感联系在一起。细节并不重要，但没有就会散掉，同另一个旅行没有什么两样。
Yes, I also made a few other products. I didn't want it to turn into an art exhibition; this would go against the whole point of the work. I wanted to do all I could to not disturb [their] daily routine, [I wanted them] to maintain their habits and rhythms. In the meantime, there must be a sense of taking part in a special event. There are a few things that are essential to have such as a bed, a sheet, a blanket, a pillow, a partition; these things make a room into a private little space. Living in this unfamiliar place in a foreign land, items that constitute historical memories and unique identities are very important, for instance 1001 chairs, luggage and USB wrist bands. These are representations of identity, and to some extent, related to self-esteem and ego. The details are not important, but they are indispensable, without them it is akin to just another travel trip.

到了卡塞尔后，所有参加《童话》的人没有任何要求和集体活动，所有人都觉得很奇怪。
When you arrive in Kassel, there are no demands made or specific activities planned for those who are participating in "Fairytale," many people find this strange.

我不希望落入这种俗套。一个作品的完整和清洁，在于原始动机和完成手段具有一致性，在于控制，不做什么比做什么更加重要。什么也不做的时候，这个作品会极大化，做任何一件事情都会使它的性质发生变化。
I don't want to fall into such conventional practices. The purity and completeness of a work depends on the original intention and being consistent in its completion; it is about control, and not doing something may be more important than actually doing something. Not doing anything will maximize the work, doing any one thing will cause its nature to change.

媒体公布的310万欧元的资金投入，如何解决资金收回的问题？
According to the press, 3.1 million Euros have been spent, how is this to be recouped?

我签了一个合约，是德文版的，到现在我也没看，我知道对我没有太大的限制。到目前为止，没有任何人查问过我资金用法的合理性，他们对我艺术创作的信任使我非常自由，我也很尊重他们的信任。回报必然是我将来要考虑的问题。
I have signed a contract, it's in German, and I haven't read it yet, but I know it won't place too many restrictions on me. Up until now no one has asked me to justify the expenses. Their trust in my artistic creation has rendered me much freedom, and I also deeply value this trust in me. Mutual consideration is certainly something I reflect on in this project.

这个项目已经实施了五天，剩下的一个月时间，你会怎样？
The project was inagurated five days ago, how will you spend the remaining month?

我觉得很久了，怎么才五天。说实话，每天挺晕的，也没有太多的想法，这个车再往前遛一段时间，该停到哪就停到哪了。
It feels like a long time has passed, how can it have only been five days? Honestly, every day is a bit dizzying and I don't give [future plans] too much thought. This car will stop somewhere after travelling for a given length of time, and it will stop at wherever it ought to stop.

《童话》为什么会引起西方媒体这么大的关注？
Why has Fairytale attracted such a great deal of attention from Western media?

第一，《童话》这个项目在Documenta是一百多个项目中比较早公开的。我在博客上招人，一下就公开了。第二，这个作品是一个可谈的问题，包括身份、选择方式、资金，都超过了通常作品的范围。参与者的状态、原因和意义，都直接涉及社会、政治、文化背景，所以就成为欧洲所有主要报纸、杂志、电视台报道的对象。报道量之大、篇幅之长和深入程度从来没有过。在网上查Documenta＃12，十条消息中有三条是关于《童话》的。朋友说从来没有一个作品得到如此多正面的报道，主要谈到作品从观念上到完成形态上的新颖和完整性。
First, of the 100 plus projects in Documenta, "Fairytale" was one of the earliest to be made public. I recruited people via my blog, so the news spread immediately. Second, the work entails hot topics of discussion including identity, choices, and funding that are beyond the usual scope of most works. The participants' [living] situations, reasons [for participating], and the significance [of the project to them] are all directly related to their social, political, and cultural backgrounds. Therefore the work has become popular with major European newspapers, magazines, and TV stations. The amount of media coverage is unprecedented both in terms of depth and reach. If you search "Documenta＃12" on the web, three out of ten results are about Fairytale. Friends told me that none of the other works have received as much positive coverage, with the focus on the originality and completeness of the work's concept and form.

同时，这也引起了西方人巨大的好奇感。
At the same time, this has also aroused a strong sense of curiosity among Westerners.

虽然我没有故意接触，但我还是遇到至少一百个人与我谈他们的感受。这个作品简单到让任何人都能够理解。老人、普通市民、卖冰激凌的人......他们都会说很喜欢，这是一个非常好的想法，有人说很有诗意。但我并不想让他们说更多，或者带有某种学术性。作品是一个媒介物，能够让不关心当代艺术的人站过来，已经让我非常吃惊。这个事情显然对他们的生活有所影响。媒体不断地希望从中获得更多的可能，但我告诉他们我没有更多的东西，他们仍然觉得不可思议。
Although I didn't intentionally search them out, I have encountered at least 100 people who have talked to me about their feelings. The work is so simple that anybody can understand it. Elderly folks and commoners, such as the ice-cream seller, have all said they really like the work, that they think it's a good idea; some have said it's very poetic. But I don't want them to say more than that, or talk about something academic. To my surprise, the work is able to act as a medium that wins people over who are normally indifferent to contemporary art. Obviously [they feel that] this topic has had some impact on their lives. The media keeps hoping to see more possibilities from it, but even though I have told them I have nothing more for them they still find it amazing.

它所波及的范围和引起的讨论，会产生怎样的影响？
What kind of influence will stem from the scope it involves and the discussions it bring about?

以我在国外生活了十几年的经验，对这个作品宣传的强度，远远超过了文化交流多年来所做的努力。他们从来没有对中国人有如此大的兴趣，谈及他们的时候会眼睛放光，带有奇异的笑容。但这些都很快会过去，最后会成为一种传说，曾经有一个疯子带着1001个疯子来到了这儿。尽管媒体一片叫好声，《童话》的意义并没有被真正理解，我必须每一次把我的话说清楚。在将来更长的时间里，人们会了解这些含义。
From my experience of living overseas for more than a decade, the force of the publicity on the work has far surpassed the efforts of cultural exchanges over the years. Before this, [the Western media and Westerners] never showed great interest in Chinese people, now their eyes beam and they smile strangely when they talk about them. But all this will pass soon, and will eventually become a legend about a crazy man bringing 1001 crazy people to this place. Even though it has been given widespread acclaim from the press, the true meaning of "Fairytale" has not been understood and I have to clarify things every time People will understand in time.

这些不同阶层的中国人群，会折射出社会层面的问题吗？
Will these Chinese people from different walks of life reflect different social issues?

《童话》是针对个人生存经验和个人觉悟的问题。标志上写着1=1001，《童话》的每个人的个人经验、个人身份、个人想象，才是最重要的，不可取代的。作品本身就是为参与的每一个人而做的，没有另外的集体意志。大多数艺术家是用众人来完成一个特殊的形态。而《童话》正相反。后来，我发现这也是《童话》能得到最广泛认同的特征之一。它并不要求艺术化，而要求把艺术生活化、常态化。这是对艺术本身的一种拷问，我也终于有机会能这样去做。
Fairytale looks into individual survival experiences and personal realizations. The sign says "1=1001", the most important aspect of Fairytale is each participant's personal experience, identity and imagination, these are irreplaceable. The work itself is created for the participants without any collective motivation. Most artists make use of crowds to complete a unique form, while Fairytale does exactly the opposite. I realized later on that, this is also one of Fairytale's features that make it such a well-recognized work. It isn't necessarily artistic, but it makes art a normal part of daily life. I finally have gotten the opportunity to do a cross-examination of art.

《童话》和你以前的作品相比，形态上有很大的变化。
In terms of form, there is a great change between Fairytale and your previous works.

从含义上有相近之处，把两种或两种以上的因素拼接，当然材料和涉及的范围完全不同。这次涉及人，艺术的生产者和接受者合一。人作为一个媒介，同时作为一个承受者、受益者或者受害者，增加了很大的复杂性。但仍然是一致地在问一些文化、价值、判断等最基本的问题。
The implications behind ["Fairytale" and previous works] are pretty close in that I have brought together two or more elements in one work. But of course, the material and the scope are totally different. This time, people are involved; it is a unification of the artist-creator and the audience. Having people play the role of medium as well as recipient, beneficiary, or victim adds to the complexities. However, it is still absolutely about questioning culture, values and judgment.

《童话》是另外一种艺术形态，人们是否可以继续相信艺术没死？
Fairytale is a different type of art form, can people continue to believe that art is not dead?

无论是社会资源、精神资源，还有对生命和文化的理解，人都是处在危机之中。希望转化，重新定义，用另外的方式，这已经是一种常态。我只是在想我个人的游戏怎么能够玩下去，或者根本不能玩下去了，人家对你的游戏方式不再有兴趣。这些都很正常。
People are constantly in crisis, whether it's regarding social or mental resources or interpretation of life and culture. Hope evolves, redefines itself, uses other methods, and this has become commonplace. I only think of how my own game can proceed, or actually if it cannot proceed, because people cease to be interested in your game plan. This is all normal.

这是你自己比较满意的作品吗？
Is this a work that you are more satisfied with?

我对我所有作品都不满意，我不是要讨好我自己，也并不是要求自己要达到。作品本身让人有一种实在感。比如说一个犯罪者，为了减轻杀自己的欲望就要去杀人。艺术家做作品是一个相似的状态。
I am never satisfied with my work, I am not trying to please myself, I am also not demanding myself to achieve anything. A work gives a sense of reality. For example, a criminal kills someone to reduce his or her desire to kill himself or herself. It is similar for an artist creating a piece of work.

你自己本人在这个作品里的位置是什么？
What role do you play in this piece of work?

我在通常的作品里是一个很主动的位置，一个确定的制作者。《童话》完全相反，我无法确定每一个参与者的状态，大多数人我不认识，即使认识，也不了解。这也是我最喜欢的部分。我的作品头一次有这么大的不确定性，发展的趋势充满可能性、不可控性和不可知性。每天我都要解决新的问题，以前根本没有想到过。我并不是一个制作人、领导的角色，我更多是在观察这个作品以什么方式变化。我是一个学习者，而且比别人更警醒。
I usually play an active role in my works as a creator who is sure of things. But it's the opposite in the case of Fairytale, I cannot be sure of each participant's condition, I don't know most of them and even if I do, I don't know them well. This is also the part that I like. For the first time, my work poses a high level of uncertainty, it's developments are full of possibilities, unpredictabilities and the unknown. I need to solve problems every day, which is something I never thought of before. I am not a creator or a leader here, I am more like an observer of the work, seeing how it evolves. I am a student, but a more alert one than other people.

《童话》本身裹挟着不同的文化、宗教和观念，你持什么观点？
Fairytale encompasses various cultures, religions and mindsets, what are your thoughts on this?

我没有什么观点。我很愿意看到一组矛盾在一起自然呈现。就像黄昏，它必然是由地域的角度、太阳的光线、云彩、空气的湿度等因素造成的。每个黄昏并没有含义，只是人们赋予了它含义。我提供的无非是这样的状态。
I don't have much opinion about this. I would like to see a group of contradictions present themselves together in a natural way. Just like a sunset, it happens as a result of geographical factors, the sun's ray, the clouds and humidity. Each sunset has no significance in itself; its significance is given by people. What I provide is such a setting.

从九十年代最早的《白皮书》和《黑皮书》时期，开始了中国当代艺术的野生状态。到2000年的"不合作方式"，中国当代艺术获得合法身份。到现在的《童话》，也许又会成为中国当代艺术占领国际舞台的转折。
Since the 1990s, the era when your early works "The White Cover Book" and "The Black Cover Book" were made, contemporary art in China began springing up all over the place. It was not until 2000, when the "Fuck Off" exhibition took place, that contemporary art in China was recognized as legitimate. And today, there is "Fairytale." Perhaps it will become a turning point for contemporary art in China taking center stage in the international scene.

我觉得一个作品能成为一个文化事件，必须具备很多独立的因素。无论是形态和语言的表述方式，还是构成的基本语汇，都要具有自我特征。《童话》尽管是一个大型作品，但仍然表现了一个地区的个人意志，它所表现的个人生存方式甚至是反国际化的。大多数参与童话的人并不了解英德语系，并不具备当代艺术经验，本身具有反文化性。这两处矛盾构成这个作品和当代世界文化、政治、经济状态相冲突的巨大核心。
I believe that in order for a piece of work to become a cultural event, it must have many independent features. From its form and verbal expression to the constitution of its basic vocabulary, it has to have distinct characteristics. Although "Fairytale" is a large-scale work, it presents the individual desires of people from a certain place, and the personal survival methods exemplified may defy internationalization. Most of the participants of Fairytale do not understand English and German, nor do they possess any experience with contemporary art, and this is anti-culture. These two contradictions make up the enormous core of the conflict between the work and the current condition of world culture, politics, and economics.

你觉得这些之间有连贯性吗？
Do you think this is all coherent?

有两个特征。第一，尽管我说我不关心，但还是表达了对文化处境和文化本身的一些怀疑；第二，都是在谈个人在大背景下的主动性和可能性，体现在非常个人化的基调上，始终在寻找一个具有个性化的方式。当然，只要你在呈现特征就是在呈现局限性。
There are two features. First, even if I say I don't care I have nonetheless expressed my doubts on cultural conditions and culture itself. Second, it's about individual initiatives and possibilities against a larger background, showing how one is in search of individualization even when we are fundamentally highly individualized. Of course, exhibiting unique characteristics means exhibiting limitations.

你对这个时代背景的判断是什么？
How do you view the current era?

我们生活在完全不同以往的全新时代，这个时代将葬送所有过去的体制、权力、话语中心，以及由此产生的一整套体系。由数字化、互联网等新的传播方式，由全球化的经济政治格局造成的结果，个人和权力体系的关系将会发生本质性的变化。同样，作为人类意识和情感表达的艺术，也面临新的语言方式。
We are living in an entirely new era very different from the past; this era will rid itself of all the organizational set-ups, power, and mainstream discourses of the past as well as the resultant system. With digitization and the Internet as means of communication and the globalized economic and political landscape, the nature of the relationship between individuals and systems of power is bound to change.

你的作品想要表达的政治立场是怎样的呢？
What is the political stand that your work aims to express?

我的政治立场，如果有的话，是个人化的，反权力的，反对由于某种利益而形成的价值标准。在世界上运转得最好的共同价值标准往往都是在某种利益的基础上完成的，大多数情况下都很简陋，没有说服力，无论是经济的，还是文化的。
My political stand, if any, is individualized, anti-power, and against values built up from specific gains. The best set of common values in the world tends to be built on specific gains, and in most cases they are unrefined and unconvincing, both economically and culturally.

能不能谈一下"门"那件作品？
Would you tell us about your work, Template?

直接谈一个作品比较好。或者继续评论《童话》这件作品，可以把它说得完整一点，要不我们什么都说，挺烦的。或者，下次专门做一次关于"门"的访谈。装置雕塑类的作品，可谈性也不是很多，没什么太大意思，就是一件东西。尽管很多人说这是文献展中最有冲击力的一件作品。但我觉得挺无聊的，那件作品是不是我的作品，我都没有太大兴趣。我觉得可能会塌掉，或者雷打了什么的，烧掉。
It's better to talk about a particular piece of work or continue to comment on Fairytale, so that it can be more thorough. It's quite a nuisance to treat everything at the same time. Maybe we can do a separate interview on "Template" another time. Basically, there is not much to talk about with installations and sculptures; they are not very meaningful, they are just things. Many people say that ["Template"] has had the greatest impact at Documenta, but I find this [type of speculation] boring. I'm not interested in whether it is my work or not. I think it will probably collapse, be struck by lightning, or burnt.

采访人：付晓东
地 点：德国卡塞尔童话驻地
2007年7月20日
Interviewer: Fu Xiao-Dong
Venue: location of Fairytale in Kassel, Germany
July 20th, 2007
snippet:
Caption: Ai Weiwei and 1 of the 5 groups that went to Kassel

作为作品的"童话"
Regarding the work "Fairytale"

为什么是1001人？
Why 1001 people?

在瑞士爬山，看到大队的意大利游人从身旁走过，拖儿带女的，我就想到带一些牵肠挂肚的中国人来做一次旅行。这更像是一个完整蛋糕切开来的一块，在这一块中包含着蛋糕所有位置的特征元素。这也是为什么不是50个或100个人的原因，数量要多到一定程度。这么多人的身份特征、年龄特征、来自几乎全部省份地区的特征，影响了文化发展交流的特征。
When I was hiking in Switzerland, I came across a huge group of Italian tourists, with their families and children in tow. The idea came to me of bringing a few Chinese people on a ...
author: Fu Xiao-Dong Translation: Eiren Shea Warneck and Ailoon Choo
